# ARCHITECTURE.md — spring-mvc-demo
*Generated by Layer 0 prompt. Reviewed and corrected manually.*

## App Identity
Minimal Spring MVC REST API that exposes a CRUD product catalog stored in memory.
No database, no external dependencies. Exists to demonstrate the Design-First methodology
on a plain Spring Boot + Web stack.

## Architecture Pattern
Single-responsibility REST API. One controller, one service, one in-memory store.
No repository layer — the service owns the `ConcurrentHashMap` directly.
No sessions. No auth. Stateless HTTP, stateful in-memory store.

## Data Flow
```
GET /api/v1/products
  → ProductController.listAll()
  → ProductService.listAll()
  → ConcurrentHashMap.values()
  → List<Product> → HTTP 200

POST /api/v1/products
  → ProductController.create(@Valid CreateProductRequest)
  → ProductService.create(request)
  → UUID.randomUUID() + new Product(id, name, price)
  → store.put(id, product)
  → Product → HTTP 201

GET /api/v1/products/{id}
  → ProductController.getById(UUID)
  → ProductService.getById(UUID)
  → store.get(id) — null → ProductNotFoundException → HTTP 404
  → Product → HTTP 200

DELETE /api/v1/products/{id}
  → ProductController.delete(UUID)
  → ProductService.delete(UUID)
  → store.remove(id) — null → ProductNotFoundException → HTTP 404
  → HTTP 204
```

## Key Components
| Component | Owns |
|-----------|------|
| `ProductController` | HTTP contract, input validation, response status |
| `ProductService` | Business logic, ConcurrentHashMap store |
| `GlobalExceptionHandler` | Exception → HTTP status mapping |
| `Product` | Domain record — id, name, price |
| `CreateProductRequest` | Validated request body — name, price |

## Folder Structure
```
src/main/java/com/example/springmvcdemo/
├── controller/    ProductController — HTTP only
├── service/       ProductService — logic + store
├── model/         Product, CreateProductRequest records
└── exception/     ProductNotFoundException, GlobalExceptionHandler, ErrorResponse
```

## Architectural Decisions
- **No repository layer:** A single in-memory map does not need an abstraction. Adding
  `ProductRepository` interface would be indirection with no benefit at this scale.
- **UUID over Long:** UUIDs avoid sequence coordination problems and are safer to expose
  in URLs — no enumeration attacks on a predictable numeric sequence.
- **ConcurrentHashMap:** Thread-safe without locking the entire service. Acceptable for
  a demo; production would use a proper database.
- **Records for model and DTO:** Immutable, concise, no boilerplate.

## Design Constraints
- Do not add a repository layer — service owns the store directly
- Do not use `Long` or `int` for IDs — UUID only
- Do not add database dependencies — in-memory only
- Do not add endpoints beyond the four defined (list, get, create, delete)
